Case Study

From the field up:
building technicians
into brand ambassadors

A learning program — eLearning and ILT development in parallel in two months — that transformed product knowledge across a national technician workforce

Client Global electronics leader
Sector Consumer electronics / HVAC
Timeline Two Months
At a glance
2mo
End-to-end delivery, from content audit to launch
90%+
Post-assessment scores across all learners
2.5hr
Self-paced eLearning module duration
2in1
eLearning and ILT built from a single asset base
The challenge

Customers were asking questions.
Technicians didn't have answers.

Customer feedback was flagging a troubling gap: service technicians could install the inverter heat pump water heater unit correctly, but struggled to speak confidently about product features, energy benefits, and safety protocols when customers asked.

For a company that views every technician as a brand ambassador, this wasn't just a training gap — it was a business problem.

  • No pre-existing course structure — content was scattered across documents, brochures, websites, and subject matter experts' heads
  • Both eLearning and ILT had to be developed concurrently and launched simultaneously
  • US regulatory frameworks required a classroom component in several states
  • Two-month delivery window — with maximum asset reuse across both modalities
The approach

Grounded in Situated Learning Theory — knowledge that sticks because it's taught in the context where it will be used.

01
Product Specifications
Components, design principles, performance characteristics, and energy efficiency features
02
Installation & Setup
Correct installation practices, configuration steps, and commissioning procedures
03
Troubleshooting & Maintenance
Fault diagnosis, maintenance routines, and safety protocol adherence
eLearning Articulate Storyline 360
  • 2.5-hour self-paced module
  • Pre- and post-assessments
  • Embedded demonstration videos
  • Accessible at the learner's own pace
  • Covers all three modules end-to-end
ILT Instructor-Led Training
  • Full-day facilitated programme
  • Participant guide + facilitator guide
  • Live installation demonstrations
  • Hands-on troubleshooting exercises
  • Designed for state-compliance contexts
The design insight
The problem we solved

Building a curriculum from scattered raw material

No single content source existed. Knowledge lived in slide decks, brochures, websites, and inside the minds of SMEs. The first design task was architecture — mapping what needed to be taught before deciding how to teach it.

What made this work

One asset base. Two complete learning experiences.

Every video, graphic, and explanation was designed for dual deployment from the start. The live demonstration videos shot for the ILT were embedded directly into the eLearning — giving online learners the same expert context as the classroom audience.

Programme in action
01
Course navigation & module overview
02
Interactive product component diagram
03
Step-by-step installation sequence
04
Troubleshooting scenario activity
05
Safety protocols knowledge check
06
Post-assessment results summary
The outcome

Technicians went into the field
ready to answer anything.

Line managers reported a marked shift in customer interactions. Customers noted that technicians could respond with confidence, clarity, and genuine product knowledge — a direct reflection of what the training had built.

"Our technicians can now answer any question a customer throws at them. That confidence — that's what this training delivered."
— Regional L&D Manager
90%+
Post-assessment scores All learners across the region scored 90% or above
2mo
On-time delivery Full programme launched within the two-month window
100%
Learner confidence All participants expressed confidence with the material on completion
2x
Modality coverage eLearning and ILT delivered from a single shared asset base
